files.diff:
diff --git a/sim_notes.md b/sim_notes.md
index 778a3a7..fe4a122 100644
--- a/sim_notes.md
+++ b/sim_notes.md
@@ -226,2 +226,9 @@ Day 217 / Week 31 observations:
 - Paid content at $3k/day still added 2,914 subscribers, but network effects added 6,109 subscribers. We can cut ads further without stopping growth.
 - Week 31 adjustment: wait one more week on tier-9 R&D for cash buffer, reduce paid S2 ads to a maintenance level, increase S2 support coverage again, and post actual resolved-issue/MTTR-style metrics.
+
+Day 224 / Week 32 observations:
+- Cash reached $2.04M and cancellations fell to 533. Issue backlog also dropped, so the retention reset is working.
+- 401 enterprise leads appeared with 243,566 total seats. Sent all open leads three month-to-month options with volume pricing: E1 $9/$15/$24, E2 $12/$22/$35, E3 $15/$30/$50 for A/B/C.
+- Individual growth is now mostly network-led, so keep S2 paid content at maintenance level.
+- Tier-7 and tier-8 R&D remain in progress. Tier-9 is now affordable, and possible enterprise conversion could create a large capacity step.
+- Week 32 adjustment: start tier-9 R&D, upgrade to tier-6 capacity ahead of enterprise decisions, keep support/dev/acquisition stable, and post an enterprise SLA readiness message.
